author: derek-larson14
description: Run Claude Code from any device via remote relay server. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Derek Larson
manifest.description: Run Claude Code from any device via remote relay server.
manifest.name: Claude Anywhere

name: Claude Anywhere

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description: Retagged from v1.0.20 (no v prefix) for Obsidian community plugin compatibility. No code changes.

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## YOLO Mode

New settings toggle to skip Claude Code permission prompts automatically. When enabled, passes `--dangerously-skip-permissions` to the Claude process.

- Add YOLO mode toggle in plugin settings
- Build claude command dynamically in startShell() based on settings
- Clean up README description
releases.name: v1.0.19: YOLO mode — skip permission prompts

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
- Show "Connecting to your Mac..." immediately — no more staring at a blank cursor
- 8s connection timeout instead of waiting 60s for the browser's internal timeout
- Scroll snapping during streaming
releases.name: v1.0.18

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
Vault path fallback for remote sessions — thanks @tsvoronos for the PR!

When mobile clients use git-based sync instead of Obsidian Sync, Claude now starts in the vault directory instead of `/`, so project-level `.claude/` settings are found correctly.
releases.name: v1.0.17

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## What's New

- **Manual server address input on mobile** — Users whose vault sync (e.g. git with `.obsidian` in `.gitignore`) doesn't propagate plugin settings can now manually enter their Mac's Tailscale address in mobile settings
- Helpful guidance text when server address isn't auto-configured, explaining where to find it on the Mac
releases.name: v1.0.16: Manual server address on mobile

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Fix Gboard voice input stripping first characters on subsequent dictations
- Clear textarea in capture phase before xterm records composition start position
releases.name: v1.0.14

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Android (Daylight DC-1): disable animations, cursor blink, and smooth scroll to prevent ghost artifacts on transflective displays
- Gboard voice: rewrite composition handler for more reliable voice-to-text input
- Simplify scroll preservation during terminal resize
releases.name: v1.0.13

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Fix Gboard voice input: Listen for 'input' events to capture voice-to-text
- Fix scroll preservation: Save and restore scroll position during textarea resize
releases.name: v1.0.12

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
Fix Tailscale detection in Electron environment

- Use `execFile` for direct binary execution instead of shell
- Use `/bin/sh` for fallback to avoid sourcing user profiles that fail in Electron's minimal environment

Thanks to @kvarnelis for the detailed bug report and fix.
releases.name: v1.0.10

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Fix Android voice input duplicating text

Changed composition handling approach for voice dictation:
- Previously: cleared textarea on compositionstart if >500ms since last input
- Now: clears textarea 100ms after compositionend, ensuring each voice session starts clean

This fixes the issue where rapid voice inputs would duplicate the last portion of text.
releases.name: v1.0.9

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Fix scroll jumping after terminal resize

Adds `scrollToBottom()` call after `fit()` to prevent terminal from jumping to incorrect scroll position during resize operations.

This fixes the issue where the terminal would "bump up" unexpectedly after running commands.
releases.name: v1.0.8

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## What's New

Added a **Default Folder** setting that lets you limit Claude's working directory to a specific subfolder of your vault.

### Features
- New "Default Folder" setting in plugin settings (e.g., `LifeOS` or `projects/work`)
- Relay server reads the setting from plugin data and applies it automatically
- Leave blank for full vault access (existing behavior)

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed crash on mobile when default folder was configured

### How It Works
Configure the setting once on your Mac in Obsidian Settings → Claude Anywhere → Default Folder. The relay server reads this setting each time a connection is made, so changes take effect on the next session.
releases.name: v1.0.7: Default Folder Setting

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## What's New

### iOS Keyboard Handling
- Container now resizes when the iOS on-screen keyboard opens, keeping controls and output visible
- Uses the `visualViewport` API for accurate keyboard detection

### iOS Custom Scrollbar
- Added custom draggable/tappable scrollbar for iOS (native scrollbars don't show on iOS)
- Wide (24px) touch targets for easy interaction
- Auto-hides after 2 seconds of no interaction

### UI Improvements
- Arrow key order changed to standard ← ↑ ↓ → layout

### Bug Fixes
- Android native scrollbar styling preserved unchanged
- Proper cleanup for all new event listeners
releases.name: v1.0.6: iOS mobile improvements

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
Improve connection error message

Clearer troubleshooting steps when mobile can't reach Mac:
- Server enabled in settings
- Obsidian open on Mac
- Tailscale connected on both devices
releases.name: v1.0.5

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Fix on-screen keyboard input on Android tablets

On-screen keyboards use IME composition for every keystroke. The previous fix for voice dictation was clearing the textarea on every `compositionstart`, which broke normal on-screen keyboard input.

**Fix**: Now tracks `compositionend` timing and only clears stale content (>500ms old), preserving rapid IME typing while still fixing voice dictation accumulation.
releases.name: v1.0.4

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes

- **Fix CSS conflict with claude-sidebar**: Use unique CSS class names and element IDs to prevent conflicts when both plugins are loaded together
- **Improve scroll position manager**: Better tracking of scroll position with bottom-following behavior
- **Mobile UX**: Increase scrollbar width to 24px for easier touch scrolling
- **Relay server improvements**: Various stability enhancements

## Install

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` and place them in your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/claude-anywhere/` folder.
releases.name: v1.0.3

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## What's New

- **Fix Android GBoard voice dictation bug**: Subsequent voice inputs no longer include stale content from previous dictations. Uses capture phase event listener to clear textarea before xterm captures composition start position.

See [xterm.js issue #3600](https://github.com/xtermjs/xterm.js/issues/3600) for background on the underlying Android/Chrome composition event issue.
releases.name: v1.0.2

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## What's New

- **Fix duplicate paste events**: External keyboards (especially on mobile) could trigger duplicate paste events. Added debounce handler to prevent this.

## Installation

Download `main.js` and `manifest.json` to your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/claude-anywhere/` folder.
releases.name: v1.0.1

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
# Claude Anywhere v1.0.0

Run Claude Code on your mobile device via Tailscale.

## Features
- Full terminal emulation with xterm.js
- WebSocket relay for remote access
- Mobile controls (arrow keys, Esc, Enter)
- Split View and Full Width modes
- Multi-session support

## Installation

See [README](https://github.com/derek-larson14/obsidian-claude-anywhere#readme) for setup instructions.

## Files
Download these files to your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/claude-anywhere/` folder:
releases.name: v1.0.0
